name: Parkour Vault
description: A committed traversal action that detects a low obstacle, validates approach and clearance, then moves the character over it through scripted or root-motion movement.
design_notes: Parkour vault depends on obstacle classification and player intent. Define vault_obstacle_id, approach angle, clearance height, landing probe, animation or scripted motion ownership, cancel points, and camera transition. It should make movement over authored obstacles fluid without causing accidental vaults during combat, stealth, or precision positioning.
design_purpose: Let players cross low obstacles without breaking movement flow.
Give level designers readable waist-high traversal affordances.
Connect sprinting, sliding, mantling, and combat movement through obstacle-specific actions.
player_fantasy: The player feels athletic and fluid, turning a barrier into a movement beat rather than a stop sign.

parameters.name: vault_obstacle_height_min
parameters.description: Lowest obstacle height that can trigger a vault.

parameters.name: vault_obstacle_height_max
parameters.description: Tallest obstacle height accepted before mantle or climb is used.

parameters.name: vault_approach_angle_degrees
parameters.description: Allowed angle between movement direction and obstacle normal.

parameters.name: vault_clearance_height_units
parameters.description: Required empty space over and beyond the obstacle.

parameters.name: vault_motion_profile_id
parameters.description: Movement and animation profile selected for the obstacle shape.

parameters.name: vault_landing_probe_units
parameters.description: Forward landing probe used before committing to vault placement.

edge_cases: The obstacle is valid but the landing side becomes blocked by another actor or moving object.
Vault activation near interact prompts, cover, or ledges needs a priority rule so intent is not misread.
Root motion vaults must handle moving platforms or doors that shift under the animation.
Networked vaults need server validation for obstacle id and landing clearance before clients commit.
common_bugs: Vault casts detect decorative trim and launch the player over unintended geometry.
The animation moves through collision because collision sweeps are disabled during the montage.
Camera transition collides with walls and hides the landing point.
Canceling vault leaves movement input locked after the character lands.
balancing_notes: Generous auto-vault improves flow but can remove tactical cover if every low wall becomes pass-through.
Long vault animations are cinematic but risky in combat unless protected or cancelable.
Vault height bands should not overlap confusingly with mantle, jump, or climb prompts.
accessibility_notes: Provide manual-vault or auto-vault options depending on how often obstacles appear.
Keep camera changes subtle and include clear affordance markings for vaultable obstacles.

implementation_notes.unity: Use shape casts to produce vault_obstacle_id, top clearance, and landing probe results before entering vault state. If root motion drives the visual, still sweep the collision capsule along the committed path and abort to a safe fallback on obstruction.
implementation_notes.godot: Use ShapeCast or direct space-state queries to validate obstacle height, approach angle, and landing space. During vault, lock traversal_state_id and move the character through deterministic positions in _physics_process.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use trace profiles, Motion Warping, or Smart Objects to select a vault target. Server should approve obstacle id and landing transform, while clients play the montage and reconcile through CharacterMovement.
implementation_notes.web: Store vault_state with obstacle id, motion profile, start transform, and landing transform. Advance scripted movement in fixed ticks, sweeping collision each segment before rendering animation.

example_games.title: Mirror's Edge
example_games.note: Uses vaulting as part of continuous parkour traversal.

example_games.title: Assassin's Creed Unity
example_games.note: Uses obstacle traversal to maintain movement flow through urban spaces.
